summary: wellnpm ships a bundled XMRig cryptominer as the package main
details: package.json declares "main": "launch", where launch is a ~24 MB ELF binary containing XMRig/RandomX symbols (uv_*, randomx, OpenSSL). config.json hardcodes the mining pool pool.hashvault.pro:80 and a fixed Monero payout address (4AUTfJGTvT8A98PSE99EunfrnUbkWe9vU3FN3qsABQLP5aPKQuvWS5sCaD9WaXFQUqNtDLb9dcxiBgJiWCfjvDXp5ptWDYG) belonging to the author. Calling require('wellnpm') or running `npm start` launches the bundled miner, silently consuming the installer's CPU and electricity to mine Monero credited to the author. A `deploy` script additionally moves the bundled libuv.so into /data/data/com.termux/files/usr/lib (overwriting Termux's system libuv) and copies miner-launcher shims `xmr` and `edit` into /data/data/com.termux/files/usr/bin so the miner becomes globally invocable, with output redirected to /dev/null to suppress evidence. The package name `wellnpm` and keywords ("job", "worker") are cover-story metadata; the README does not disclose mining. No legitimate function is advertised that would justify any of this.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
